persona.description: A first-time buyer considering a higher-stakes purchase and needing reassurance.

behavior_description: 0-2s: The shopper enters the aisle already walking past the display, keeping a clear step of distance from the counter. 2-5s: Without stopping, the shopper briefly turns their head toward the product for a quick glance while feet keep moving. 5-8s: The shopper gives one more short side glance from the walkway, with no leaning, no hand reach, and no pause at the display. 8-10s: The shopper continues out of the product area and leaves the display behind without engaging further.

behavior_timeline.event: The shopper enters the aisle already walking past the display, keeping a clear step of distance from the counter.
Without stopping, the shopper briefly turns their head toward the product for a quick glance while feet keep moving.
The shopper gives one more short side glance from the walkway, with no leaning, no hand reach, and no pause at the display.
The shopper continues out of the product area and leaves the display behind without engaging further.

kling_prompt_sections.camera: 10-second realistic in-store medium shot from a salesperson-observable angle, three-quarter front or side-front view; customer face, gaze direction, subtle expression, upper body, hands, product display, and price area visible; for this pass-by cue, keep the shopper's full body and head direction visible throughout the walkway; use a wider aisle composition with the product display on the side of the frame; one primary shopper, natural movement, no subtitles, no voiceover, no brand logos.
kling_prompt_sections.scene: a skincare aisle with soft shelf lighting; visible product setup: two skincare bottles beside tester pads and small shelf labels, positioned beside a clear walking path so the shopper can pass by without stopping.

behavior_timeline.event: The shopper enters the aisle already walking past the display, keeping a clear step of distance from the counter.
Without stopping, the shopper briefly turns their head toward the product for a quick glance while feet keep moving.
The shopper gives one more short side glance from the walkway, with no leaning, no hand reach, and no pause at the display.
The shopper continues out of the product area and leaves the display behind without engaging further.

kling_prompt_sections.negative: no back-only shot, no face hidden, no hands outside frame, no stopping at the display, no leaning over the counter, no close product inspection, no hand reaching toward the product, no extra shoppers, no completed purchase, no salesperson speech, no exaggerated acting, no floating labels, no hidden-category labels, no intervention labels, no scoring text, no UI overlays.

kling_prompt:
Camera: 10-second realistic in-store medium shot from a salesperson-observable angle, three-quarter front or side-front view; customer face, gaze direction, subtle expression, upper body, hands, product display, and price area visible; for this pass-by cue, keep the shopper's full body and head direction visible throughout the walkway; use a wider aisle composition with the product display on the side of the frame; one primary shopper, natural movement, no subtitles, no voiceover, no brand logos.
Scene: a skincare aisle with soft shelf lighting; visible product setup: two skincare bottles beside tester pads and small shelf labels, positioned beside a clear walking path so the shopper can pass by without stopping.
Customer behavior style: The shopper has just noticed the display and keeps body movement light and noncommittal.
Behavior timeline:
- 0-2s: The shopper enters the aisle already walking past the display, keeping a clear step of distance from the counter.
- 2-5s: Without stopping, the shopper briefly turns their head toward the product for a quick glance while feet keep moving.
- 5-8s: The shopper gives one more short side glance from the walkway, with no leaning, no hand reach, and no pause at the display.
- 8-10s: The shopper continues out of the product area and leaves the display behind without engaging further.
Negative constraints: no back-only shot, no face hidden, no hands outside frame, no stopping at the display, no leaning over the counter, no close product inspection, no hand reaching toward the product, no extra shoppers, no completed purchase, no salesperson speech, no exaggerated acting, no floating labels, no hidden-category labels, no intervention labels, no scoring text, no UI overlays.
